author: śrīla sanātana gosvāmī
verses:
śrī⋅jagannātha nīlādri śiromukuṭa⋅ratna he
dārubrahman ghanaśyāma prasīda puruṣottama

praphulla⋅puṇḍarīkākṣa lavaṇāvdhi taṭāmṛta
guṭikodara māṁ pāhi nānābhoga⋅purandara

nijādhara⋅sudhādāyinnindradyumna⋅prasādita
subhadrā⋅pālana⋅vyagra rāmānuja namo‛stu te

guṇḍicā⋅rathayātrādi⋅mahotsava⋅vivardhana
bhaktavatsala vande tvāṁ guṇḍicārathamaṇḍanam

dīnahīna⋅mahānīca⋅dayārdrīkṛta⋅mānasa
nitya⋅nūtana⋅māhātmyadarśin caitanyavallabha
en_translation:
O Śrī Jagannātha, jewel on the crown of the Blue Mountain! O wooden Brahman, dark-blue like a cloud, O Supreme Person, please be kind to me.

O You whose eyes are like fully bloomed lotus flowers, O nectar on the seashore! O pot-bellied One, please protect me, O provider of various enjoyments.

O Giver of the nectar of Your own lips, You who were pleased by King Indradyumna! O You who are eager to protect Subhadrā, O younger brother of Balarāma, salutations to You.

O Enhancer of great festivals like the Guṇḍicā chariot festival! O You who are affectionate to Your devotees, I bow to You, the adornment of the Guṇḍicā chariot.

O You whose mind is softened with compassion for the poor and the most fallen! O Revealer of ever-new glories, O beloved of Lord Caitanya!
